topic: China termination payroll runbook for foreign employers: severance, final payroll, IIT and social insurance offboarding controls
angle: Operations-first playbook for HR and payroll teams handling employee exits in China with auditable controls and deadline discipline

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China (2012 Amendment)
sources.notes: Used for termination conditions, severance framework, and employer obligations around ending labor contracts.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
sources.notes: Used for employer obligations to register, contribute, and administer social insurance status changes linked to employee exits.

sources.title: Worldwide Tax Summaries – People's Republic of China: Taxes on personal income
sources.notes: Used for practical IIT withholding context and termination-payment tax handling considerations for payroll operations.

sources.title: Worldwide Tax Summaries – People's Republic of China: Other taxes
sources.notes: Used for social security and housing fund practical context affecting final-pay and offboarding process controls.

consensus_points: Termination in China is process-sensitive: legal basis, evidence quality, and payment timing all affect dispute exposure.
Severance and final payroll must be treated as one controlled workflow across HR, payroll, and line management rather than isolated tasks.
IIT and social-insurance treatment can involve city-level implementation differences; local confirmation is required for edge cases.
uncertainties_or_interpretation_risks: Local bureau practice can differ on exact document forms, filing windows, and treatment of specific final-payment components.
Certain termination-payment tax treatments may depend on current implementing circulars and local system logic not fully reflected in high-level English summaries.
Chinese-language original rules and city-specific guidance prevail where English translations are simplified or outdated.
